To manage the detail panel, set up the app shell layout so the main view can open inside the panel views alongside dialogs and confirmation messages, as laid out in this package from the project.

import type React from "react"

export interface PanelData {
  id: string
  content: React.ReactNode
}

export interface PanelsProps extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement> {
  children: React.ReactNode
  calculatedPanels: CalculatedPanelState[]
  displayMode: "docked" | "overlay"
  totalVisibleWidth: number
  removePanel: (panelId: string) => void
  activateStackedPanel: (panelId: string) => void
  mainViewMinWidth: number
  containerWidth: number
}

export interface CalculatedPanelState {
  id: string
  panelType: string
  content: React.ReactNode
  mode: "docked" | "overlay"
  state: "full" | "stacked" | "hidden"
  position: {
    right: number
    zIndex: number
    width: number
  }
}

export interface UsePanelsReturn {
  calculatedPanels: CalculatedPanelState[]
  displayMode: "docked" | "overlay"
  totalVisibleWidth: number
  activePanelId: string | null
  removePanel: (panelId: string) => void
  activateStackedPanel: (panelId: string) => void
}

export type PanelsConfigProps = {
  panelWidth?: number
  maxPanels?: number
  maxVisiblePanels?: number
  mainViewMinWidth?: number
  breakpoint?: "sm" | "md" | "lg" | "xl" | "2xl"
  stackedPanelWidth?: number
  onDockedPanelsChange?: (dockedPanelIds: string[]) => void
}

export interface UsePanelsArgs extends PanelsConfigProps {
  panels: PanelData[]
  activePanelId: string | null
  containerWidth: number
  onClose: (id: string) => void
  onActivateRequest: (id: string) => void
}

export type UseDetailPanelsArgs = UsePanelsArgs
export type UseDetailPanelsReturn = UsePanelsReturn
export type DetailPanelsProps = PanelsConfigProps

import { createContext, useContext } from "react"
import type { ComponentType } from "react"

import type { SerializableProps } from "./persistence"
import type { UsePanelsArgs, CalculatedPanelState } from "./types"

export interface PanelsContextType<
  TPanelRegistry extends Record<string, ComponentType<unknown>> = Record<
    string,
    ComponentType<unknown>
  >,
> {
  calculatedPanels: CalculatedPanelState[]
  displayMode: "docked" | "overlay"
  totalVisibleWidth: number
  containerWidth: number
  panelWidth?: number
  maxPanels?: number
  maxVisiblePanels?: number
  mainViewMinWidth?: number
  breakpoint?: UsePanelsArgs["breakpoint"]
  stackedPanelWidth?: number
  activePanelType: string | null
  getPanelProps: (type: keyof TPanelRegistry) => SerializableProps | undefined
  openPanel: <TType extends keyof TPanelRegistry>(type: TType, props?: SerializableProps) => void
  removePanel: (type: keyof TPanelRegistry | string) => void
  activateStackedPanel: (type: keyof TPanelRegistry | string) => void
  onDockedPanelsChange?: (dockedPanelIds: string[]) => void
}

const defaultContextValue: PanelsContextType = {
  calculatedPanels: [],
  displayMode: "docked",
  totalVisibleWidth: 0,
  removePanel: () => {
    console.warn("removePanel called outside Provider")
  },
  activateStackedPanel: () => {
    console.warn("activateStackedPanel called outside Provider")
  },
  openPanel: () => {
    console.warn("openPanel called outside Provider")
  },
  getPanelProps: () => {
    console.warn("getPanelProps called outside Provider")
    return undefined
  },
  containerWidth: 0,
  activePanelType: null,
  panelWidth: undefined,
  maxPanels: undefined,
  maxVisiblePanels: undefined,
  mainViewMinWidth: undefined,
  breakpoint: undefined,
  stackedPanelWidth: undefined,
  onDockedPanelsChange: () => {
    console.warn("onDockedPanelsChange called outside Provider")
  },
}

export const PanelsContext = createContext<PanelsContextType>(defaultContextValue)

export function usePanelsContext<
  TPanelRegistry extends Record<string, ComponentType<unknown>> = Record<
    string,
    ComponentType<unknown>
  >,
>(): PanelsContextType<TPanelRegistry> {
  return useContext(PanelsContext) as PanelsContextType<TPanelRegistry>
}

content.text:
Here is what was implemented and how it fits together.

### `@camping/ui` (ported sewdn + shell)
- **`packages/ui/src/components/sewdn/`** — `Panels` / `PanelsLayout` / `PanelsProvider`, `CoverView` (+ scroll hook), `CardList` with **`cardDiskGridClassName`** and **`CardDisk`** alias, `IconButton`, `Scrim`, `Toolbar`, **`AppShell`** (flex + `Toaster`), **`AppProvider`** + **`useApp`** (confirm/alert + imperative dialog).
- **`packages/ui/src/components/ui/`** — `alert-dialog.tsx`, `alert-dialog-provider.tsx`, `dialog-provider.tsx`, `side-panel-surface.ts`, and **`ScrollArea`** `viewportRef` (needed by `CoverView`).
- **`framer-motion`** added on `@camping/ui`; barrel export via `packages/ui/src/index.ts` (`export * from "./components/sewdn"`).

### App shell & explore
- **`apps/frontend-web/src/components/AppChrome.tsx`** — Full-height column: top bar → **`AppShell`** → **`AppProvider`** → scrollable main (with bottom nav padding) so **dialogs, confirms, toasts, and panels** share one subtree.
- **`apps/frontend-web/src/routes/explore.tsx`** — **`PanelsLayout`** + `explorePanelRegistry`, **`ExploreSurfMap`** (Leaflet + OSM, **hover tooltips**, **click** → `openPanel("surfSchoolDetail", { slug })`), existing chips / `MapPeekCard` kept below.
- **`apps/frontend-web/src/components/explore-panels.tsx`** — **`CoverView`** panels: surf school (external **website** + optional **Maps**, **favorites**, **nearby camping** → opens camping panel) and camping spot (link, favorites, **nearby surf schools**).
- **`apps/frontend-web/src/lib/geo.ts`** & **`nearby.ts`** — Haversine + “same region first, then distance” neighbors (within ~120 km, up to 8).

### Card disk / responsive columns
- **`CardList`** / **`CardDisk`** / **`cardDiskGridClassName`** from `@camping/ui`.
- **`packages/ui-lib/src/camping/region-list.tsx`** & **`packages/ui-lib/src/components/region-grid/RegionGrid.tsx`** — Use the same **container-query grid** as the sewdn card disk.
- **`apps/frontend-web/src/routes/schools.tsx`**, **`saved.tsx`** — Lists use **`CardList`**.

### Dependencies
- **`apps/frontend-web`**: `leaflet`, `react-leaflet`, `@types/leaflet`.

`bun run --filter @camping/ui build`, `@camping/ui-lib build`, and `@camping/frontend-web build` all succeed; Biome `--write` was applied on the touched frontend files.

**Note:** Only **one** surf-school detail and **one** camping detail panel type can be open at a time (panel id = panel type). Opening another school replaces the surf panel; stacking still works between **surf** and **camping** panel types. If you need multiple schools open at once, we’d need composite panel ids (e.g. slug in the type key) and a dynamic registry.

content.text:
Here’s what changed to mirror the onboarding app’s `panels/` layout.

### New folder: `apps/frontend-web/src/panels/`

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| **`panel-config.ts`** | Panel id constants (`SURF_SCHOOL_DETAIL_PANEL_TYPE`, `CAMPING_SPOT_DETAIL_PANEL_TYPE`), cover image URL, `regionLabel()`. |
| **`panel-registry-types.ts`** | `AppPanelRegistry` + `ExploreDetailPanelProps` (no imports from panel components → avoids cycles). |
| **`panelRegistry.ts`** | `panelRegistry` object + `satisfies AppPanelRegistry`; re-exports `AppPanelRegistry`. |
| **`panelsLayout.tsx`** | `ExplorePanelsLayout` wrapping `PanelsLayout` with `panelTypes={panelRegistry}` and `panelWidth={520}` (same idea as `EuroCannicPanelsLayout`). Also exported as **`AppPanelsLayout`** like the example. |
| **`useExplorePanels.ts`** | Typed `usePanelsContext<AppPanelRegistry>()` (same idea as `useAppPanels`). |
| **`panel-chrome.tsx`** | Shared `CloseGlyph` for panel headers. |
| **`surf_school_detail_panel.tsx`** / **`camping_spot_detail_panel.tsx`** | Detail UI (moved from the old `explore-panels.tsx`). |
| **`index.ts`** | Barrel exports. |

### Route updates

- **`src/routes/explore.tsx`** — Uses `ExplorePanelsLayout` and `useExplorePanels()` + `SURF_SCHOOL_DETAIL_PANEL_TYPE` for `openPanel`; no inline `PanelsLayout` / registry.
- **`src/components/explore-panels.tsx`** — Removed (logic lives under `panels/`).

TanStack Router is unchanged (no `useUrlQueryPersistence`); you can add URL-backed persistence later in `panelsLayout.tsx` if you introduce a small adapter.

Build and Biome checks for `src/panels` and `explore.tsx` are clean.

content.text:
Here’s what changed:

### `apps/frontend-web/src/routes/explore.tsx`
- Removed filters, badges, `MapPeekCard`, region navigation, `AppLayout`, and all extra copy.
- The route is only **`ExplorePanelsLayout` → `ExploreSurfMap`**, with `openPanel` on marker click.

### `apps/frontend-web/src/components/explore-map.tsx`
- Map fills the **remaining flex space**: outer shell uses `flex flex-1 min-h-0 w-full min-w-0 flex-col` (no fixed `55dvh`, no rounded “card” frame).
- **`MapResizeSync`** — `ResizeObserver` + `invalidateSize()` so Leaflet tracks the real size after layout (and when the side panel opens/closes).
- **`MapContainer`** — `style={{ height: "100%", width: "100%" }}` and utility classes so `.leaflet-container` is full width/height of the shell.

### `apps/frontend-web/src/panels/panelsLayout.tsx`
- **`PanelsLayout`** `className` extended with **`h-full min-h-0 min-w-0 flex`** so the panel row participates in the chrome’s flex height chain and the main column can grow to full height.

Together with existing **`AppChrome`** (`min-h-dvh`, inner `flex-1 min-h-0`, bottom padding for the nav), `/explore` is now **top bar → full-bleed map → bottom nav**, with detail panels opening from the map as before.

Here is what was implemented.

### 1. `@camping/ui` — `use-url-query-detail-panels.ts`
Same URL contract as Procertus: query keys `panels`, `activePanel`, `panelProps` (props JSON in the query string). The module exports:

- `PANELS_URL_QUERY_KEYS` — string literals for those keys  
- `parsePanelsUrlSearch` — parse from a search-shaped object  
- `mergePanelsIntoSearch` — merge panel state into an existing search object so other params stay intact  

It is re-exported from `packages/ui/src/components/sewdn/Panels/index.ts`.

### 2. `apps/frontend-web` — TanStack URL persistence
- **`src/panels/explore-url-search.ts`** — `ExplorePanelsSearch` + `validateExplorePanelsSearch` for the `/explore` route.  
- **`src/panels/useExploreUrlPersistence.ts`** — `PersistenceLayer` using `getRouteApi("/explore")`, `parsePanelsUrlSearch`, and `mergePanelsIntoSearch` with `navigate({ search: (prev) => …, replace: true })`.  
- **`src/routes/explore.tsx`** — `validateSearch: validateExplorePanelsSearch`.  
- **`src/panels/panelsLayout.tsx`** — `persistenceLayer={useExploreUrlPersistence()}`.

Procertus onboarding uses **`useUrlQueryPersistence`** (React Router); here the same behavior is **`useExploreUrlPersistence`** (TanStack), backed by the shared helpers in the new `use-url-query-detail-panels` module so query names and parse/merge logic stay aligned with the example project.

`bun run --filter @camping/ui build` and `bun run --filter @camping/frontend-web build` both succeed; Biome was run with `--write` on the touched frontend panel/route files only.

content.text:
Here is what was going wrong and what we changed.

### Cause
The explore stack leaned on **`h-full` / `height: 100%`** all the way from `PanelsLayout` → main slot → map. In a **column flex** layout, percentage height often **does not resolve** when parents only get height from **`flex-1`** / **`min-h-dvh`**, so the map’s box could end up with **no usable height** even though the layout looked “full viewport.”

### Fixes
1. **`Panels.tsx` (@camping/ui)**  
   - Outer layout: default **`min-h-0`** so flex children can shrink and pass height down.  
   - Main slot: **`h-full` removed** → **`flex min-h-0 min-w-0 flex-col`** so height comes from flex **stretch**, not `%`.  
   - Docked panel rail: **`h-full`** → **`min-h-0 self-stretch`** so it still fills the row cross-axis without relying on a broken `%` chain.

2. **`panelsLayout.tsx`**  
   - Dropped **`h-full`** on `PanelsLayout`; kept **`flex min-h-0 min-w-0 flex-1`**.

3. **`explore.tsx`**  
   - **`ExploreMain`**: `h-full` removed (same idea).  
   - **`ExploreRoute`**: wrapped in **`flex min-h-0 flex-1 flex-col`** so the explore tree is an explicit flex child under `AppChrome`.

4. **`explore-map.tsx`**  
   - **`MapResizeSync`** now observes **`map.getContainer()`** (the element whose size actually changes), not only its parent.  
   - **`MapContainer`**: **`relative`**, **`flex-1 min-h-0 w-full`**, and **`minHeight: 0`** in `style` so Leaflet gets a real box inside the flex column.

`bun run --filter @camping/ui build` and `bun run --filter @camping/frontend-web build` both pass. After a refresh, `/explore` should show the map again with the same full-height intent, but driven by **`flex-1` + `min-h-0`** instead of **`h-full`**.

content.text:
Here is what was added.

### Behavior
- **`useExploreRegionFilter`** (`apps/frontend-web/src/hooks/use-explore-region-filter.ts`): reads/writes **`localStorage`** key `camping.frontend-web.explore.selectedRegionSlugs` as a **JSON string array** of region slugs (sorted, deduped, restricted to known slugs when the region list changes).
- **No selection stored (`[]`)** = *no region filter* → all surf schools and all geocoded camping spots (with coordinates) stay visible.
- **Non-empty selection** = show a school or campsite only if its **`regionSlug`** is in that set. Entries **without** `regionSlug` are **hidden** while a filter is active (they cannot be matched to a region).

### UI
- **`ExploreRegionFilterMenu`** (`apps/frontend-web/src/components/explore-region-filter-menu.tsx`): **Regions** popover (next to Surf schools / Camping) with a scrollable checkbox list (sorted region names), **`Show all regions`** (clears to `[]` and persists), and **`Select all regions`** (stores every known slug; disables when that full set is already selected). Trigger shows **`(n)`** when a filter is active.

### Map wiring
- **`explore.tsx`**: loads **`getRegions()`**, sorts by name, passes **`regions`** into the map.
- **`explore-map.tsx`**: derives **`schoolsOnMap`** / **`campingOnMap`** with `passesRegionFilter`; markers and **`FitVisibleBounds`** use those lists.

`passesExploreRegionFilter` is exported from the hook file for reuse or tests. Build and Biome checks pass.

content.text:
Here is what was implemented.

### `CoverView` (`packages/ui/.../CoverView/CoverView.tsx`)
- New optional prop **`coverIconAction`** — rendered on the **right** of the cover toolbar with **`secondaryAction`** (desktop: one `motion.div` with `gap-1`; mobile: same grouping in the top bar).
- Toolbar visibility when **`coverIconAction`** alone is set matches the updated condition.

### `useCoverViewScroll`
- Renamed the scroll flag to **`showRightToolbarActions`** (`true` when `secondaryAction` or `coverIconAction` is present) so title padding still clears the right controls.

### Saved / favorites (reactive + shared)
- **`useUserOverlay`** is now backed by **`UserOverlayProvider`** in the same file: one React state tree for the whole app, **`persist`** updates everyone, and a **`storage`** listener reapplies `localStorage` when another tab changes `camping:user-camping-state-v1`.
- **`AppChrome`** wraps route content with **`UserOverlayProvider`** (inside **`AppProvider`**). **`useUserOverlay()`** throws if used outside the provider.
- Provider return uses **`createElement`** so the file can stay **`.ts`**.

### Detail panels
- **`camping_spot_detail_panel`** / **`surf_school_detail_panel`**: **`coverIconAction`** is a heart **`IconButton`** (`invertColors`, `aria-pressed`, filled icon when saved) calling **`overlay.toggleFavorite`** for **`campingSpot`** / **`surfSchool`**.
- Inline **`FavoriteButton`** in the scroll body was removed so saving is only in the cover toolbar.

Toggling the heart updates **`localStorage`**, the open panel’s icon (same context), and other routes (e.g. Saved) without a full reload; other tabs pick up changes via **`storage`**.

content.text:
Here is what changed.

### Cause
`surfSchoolRelatedCampingOptions` merged **all curated (`relatedSurfSchoolSlugs`) campsites first**, then filled with geographic neighbors. `Map` insertion order meant **distance was not the global sort**. Surf schools on the campsite panel were ordered **same region before distance**, so nearer schools in another region could rank lower.

### Changes (`apps/frontend-web/src/lib/nearby.ts`)
- **`surfSchoolRelatedCampingOptionsWithDistance`** — One pass over all spots: include if **linked to the school** or **≤ 120 km** (with coordinates). Each row gets **`distanceKm`** (`Infinity` if no coords). **Sort by `distanceKm`**, then name, then **`slice(8)`**.
- **`nearbySchoolsForCampingWithDistance`** — Same idea: **`distanceKm`**, sort **by distance only** (name tie-break), cap at 8.
- **`surfSchoolRelatedCampingOptions`** / **`nearbySchoolsForCamping`** — Still exported; they map the `…WithDistance` results to plain entities.

### Panels
- **`useMemo`** still depends on `school`/`spot` and catalog lists; it now calls the `…WithDistance` helpers so **sorting + distances are computed once per memo**.
- **`CardList` items** include **`distanceKm`** so the subtitle does not call **`haversineKm`** again in the row renderer.

Note: the **campsite** detail sheet only has a **“Surf schools nearby”** block (no second campsite list). That block is now **nearest surf schools first** by km. If you later add “other campsites nearby,” the same `…WithDistance` pattern applies.

`bun run build` in `apps/frontend-web` succeeds after these edits.

Typecheck packages/ui after adding vite plugin file

content.text:
Here is what was wrong and what we aligned with the Procertus pattern.

### Issues
1. **`@camping/ui/globals.css?url` in `head`** — Treating the design-system CSS as a bare URL can sidestep the normal Vite + **`@tailwindcss/vite`** pipeline compared to a **side-effect CSS import** that is bundled like in PT1’s `main.tsx` → `./index.css`.
2. **No app-level CSS entry** — PT1 uses `index.css` with `@import "@procertus-ui/ui/globals.css"` and **`@source "./**/*.{ts,tsx}"`** so Tailwind scans the **app** from the **app Vite root**. Camping had **`@source "../../../../apps/frontend-web/src"`** inside **`packages/ui`**, which is brittle and wrong for multiple apps.
3. **No `@source ".."` on `globals.css`** — PT1 scans **`packages/ui/src`** explicitly so utilities resolve when the CSS entry is resolved from another project root.
4. **`@/` in `packages/ui`** — With `tsconfigPaths` from **`apps/frontend-web`**, `@/*` points at the **app** `src`. Shadcn code under **`@camping/ui`** expects `@/` → **`packages/ui/src`**. PT1 fixes this with **`workspaceUiAliasPlugin`**; the same plugin is now in the camping repo.

### Changes
| Area | Change |
|------|--------|
| **`apps/frontend-web/src/index.css`** (new) | `@import "@camping/ui/globals.css"` + `@source "./**/*.{ts,tsx}"` (same idea as PT1). |
| **`apps/frontend-web/src/routes/__root.tsx`** | `import "../index.css"`; removed `globals.css?url` and the `<link rel="stylesheet">`. |
| **`packages/ui/src/styles/globals.css`** | Added **`@source ".."`** (scan `packages/ui/src`); removed the hardcoded **`apps/frontend-web`** path. Kept **`@source` for `ui-lib`**. |
| **`packages/ui/src/vite-workspace-ui-alias.ts`** (new) | Copy of PT1’s resolver; plugin name **`camping-ui-workspace-at-alias`**. |
| **`apps/frontend-web/vite.config.ts`** | Plugin runs **first**; **`server.fs.allow`** monorepo root; **`dedupe`** for `react`, `react-dom`, `@camping/ui`. |
| **`packages/ui/package.json`** | Export **`./vite-workspace-ui-alias`** for reuse (e.g. Storybook). |
| **`apps/frontend-web/README.md`** | Documents **`src/index.css`** as the styling entry. |

`bun run build` in **`apps/frontend-web`** and **`bun run build`** in **`packages/ui`** both succeed. The main client CSS chunk is now the processed **`index-*.css`** (~167 kB), which indicates Tailwind is compiling through the app entry as intended.

**Adding another app later:** give it its own `src/index.css` with the same `@import` + `@source "./**/*.{ts,tsx}"`, and wire the same **`workspaceUiAliasPlugin`** in that app’s Vite config—no need to edit **`packages/ui`** `@source` for each app.

Here is what was implemented.

### URL → highlight
In `explore.tsx`, **`useExploreMapHighlight`** reads the same search shape as `useExploreUrlPersistence` via **`parsePanelsUrlSearch`**, then picks the **focused** panel type:

1. **`activePanel`** from the URL when set

For that type, if it is **`surfSchoolDetail`** or **`campingSpotDetail`**, it reads **`slug`** from **`panelProps`** and passes **`highlightedSchoolSlug`** / **`highlightedCampingSlug`** into the map.

### Map behavior (`explore-map.tsx`)
- New optional props: **`highlightedSchoolSlug`**, **`highlightedCampingSlug`**.
- Markers are split into **inactive** vs **active**; the **active** marker is rendered **after** the inactive ones so it sits **on top** in Leaflet.
- **Active** styling: larger radius, thicker stroke, brighter fill (orange outline on both school and camping highlights).

Opening a spot from a school panel (or the reverse) updates the URL as before; the map now follows that state, including deep links or reloads with `panels` / `activePanel` / `panelProps` already set.

If you later want **two** markers styled when two detail panels are open but only one is “active”, we can add a softer “stacked open” style for the non-active slug from **`openPanelTypes`**.

input.description: Verify build after fixing panels and saved route

content.text:
Here is what was wrong and what we changed.

### Issues
1. **`toggleFavorite` read `state` from a closure** — Updates could race or batch badly; favorites should use a **functional `setState`** so each toggle always reads the latest `prev` favorites.
2. **`isFavorite` used `encodeEntityRefKey` while toggles used `favoriteKey`** — They are the same today, but **`favoriteKey`** is the documented key for favorites; **`isFavorite`** now uses **`favoriteKey`** only.
3. **`persist` wrote `localStorage` synchronously next to `setState`** — Splitting concerns: **React state is the source of truth**, and a dedicated **`useEffect([state, hydrated])`** writes **`camping:user-camping-state-v1`** after hydration so the first paint does not overwrite stored data with an empty object.
4. **`/saved` memo deps were `[overlay]`** — The list did not always recompute when favorites changed in a way hooks could optimize; deps are now **`overlay.state`** and **`overlay.isFavorite`** so saved lists track favorites reliably.
5. **No small “mutation” API for hearts** — Added **`useFavoriteToggle(type, id)`** returning **`{ saved, mutateToggle, hydrated }`** for stable **`onClick={mutateToggle}`** and **`disabled={!hydrated}`** so saves do not run before the client has loaded from `localStorage`.

### API surface (`use-user-overlay.tsx`)
- **`USER_CAMPING_STATE_STORAGE_KEY`** — exported constant for the localStorage key.
- **`UserOverlayApi`** — JSDoc clarifies localStorage-backed behavior and alignment with **`UserOverlayStorePort`** in `@camping/domain-shared`.
- **`useFavoriteToggle(entityKind, entityId)`** — mutation-style hook for save / heart controls.
- **`toggleFavorite` / draft / review mutators** — use **functional `setState`** where they derive from previous state; **`persist(next)`** only updates React state; the **sync effect** persists to disk.

### Panels
- **`surf_school_detail_panel.tsx`** and **`camping_spot_detail_panel.tsx`** use **`useFavoriteToggle(...)`**, **`onClick={mutateToggle}`**, and **`disabled={!hydrated}`** on the heart **`IconButton`**.

You can confirm in DevTools → Application → Local Storage → key **`camping:user-camping-state-v1`** (same as **`USER_CAMPING_STATE_STORAGE_KEY`**) after toggling a heart on `/explore` detail panels or on **`/saved`**.

[REDACTED]

Identifying the issue: `UserCampingState` is imported as a type-only import while the code uses `new UserCampingState(...)` at runtime. Importing the class as a value.

content.text:
**Cause:** `UserCampingState` was imported with `type UserCampingState`, so the bundler only kept it for TypeScript and **stripped it from the JS output**. The hook still runs `new UserCampingState(...)`, so at runtime the engine looks up a variable that was never emitted → **“Can't find variable: UserCampingState”**.

**Change:** Import the Effect **class** as a runtime value:

```ts
UserCampingState,
```

instead of `type UserCampingState`. You can still use `UserCampingState` in type positions with that import. Build succeeds after the fix.
